configuration: fully local qwen2.5:7b lane (MASE_LOCAL_ONLY=1, cloud blocked): scripts/run_lme_full.py preset - multipass + HyDE + rerank, local verifier, qtype routing on, qid routing disabled, LLM judge disabled; 60.6min wall on dual RTX 3090

llm_judge.judge_model: deepseek-chat (anthropic-compatible endpoint), conservative FAIL->PASS only, 33 upgrades, 0 judge-unavailable, 2026-07-08
llm_judge.judge_caveat: different judge model than the April cloud lane (kimi-k2.5 chain); cross-lane judge deltas are not directly comparable

lane_semantics.substring_floor_only: LLM-judge disabled by design in this local lane; substring is the never-wrong floor. The 2026-04 cloud lane showed judge upgrades of ~19pp on the same benchmark; a judge re-score of this run requires cloud calls and is pending.
lane_semantics.cross_lane_comparison_caveat: The 2026-04 published lanes ran a GLM-5 cloud executor; this lane runs qwen2.5:7b fully local with a 12k-char local fact-sheet cap. Numbers are lane-local baselines, not an executor-for-executor comparison. For reference: cloud published substring 61.0, cloud best-stable substring 75.4.

notes: First committed fully-local LongMemEval-S 500 baseline on v0.16: 62.60% substring floor, exceeding the 2026-04 cloud published substring lane (61.0) despite the 7B local executor.
knowledge-update (47.44%) is the weakest type in this lane; temporal-reasoning (59.4%) matches the known weakness from the cloud-lane forensics.
